title: Seven Sinaloa Meth Traffickers Sentenced in Kansas City; Leaders Receive 25-Year Terms
detail: A US federal court sentenced seven individuals — most of them undocumented Mexican nationals — on April 29, 2026 for operating a Sinaloa Cartel-linked methamphetamine trafficking network that ran from Sinaloa state to Kansas City, Missouri. Lead defendants Jose Santos Macias Roman and Juan Ramirez Gonzalez each received 300-month (25-year) prison terms. The network transported liquid methamphetamine from Sinaloa across the border and converted it to crystal meth at distribution sites in Kansas City for retail distribution across the Midwest. Investigators documented the operation from January 2020 through mid-2023, ultimately seizing more than 70 kilograms of methamphetamine, $35,000 in cash, and three firearms. The case illustrates how the Sinaloa Cartel's meth distribution infrastructure in the US interior continues to face dismantlement despite the ongoing internal faction war — though the Kansas City network predates the September 2024 Chapitos-Mayoza split. The sentencings come as the conflict has disrupted Sinaloa-to-US trafficking routes: SEDENA Secretary Trevilla Trejo confirmed the cartel has lost 30 of 42 trafficking routes it previously dominated. Whether the Kansas City network was Chapitos- or Mayiza-affiliated was not specified in court documents.

media.caption: Seven Sinaloa meth traffickers sentenced in Kansas City on April 29, 2026 — leaders receive 25 years for transporting liquid meth from Sinaloa and converting to crystal for Midwest distribution

title: CJNG Commander 'El Jardinero' Captured in Nayarit — El Mencho's Security Chief and Potential Successor Detained
detail: Mexican security forces captured Audias Flores Silva — known as 'El Jardinero' ('The Gardener') — a top CJNG commander with a $5 million US reward, on April 29, 2026 after he was found hiding in a roadside ditch near El Mirador, Nayarit. The operation deployed over 100 troops, six helicopters, and four aircraft. El Jardinero served as El Mencho's head of security and was considered a leading candidate to succeed Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes (El Mencho) after El Mencho's death in a Mexican military operation in February 2026. CJNG subsequently confirmed Juan Carlos Valencia González ('El 03') — El Mencho's stepson — as the organization's new leader on April 6, 2026, but Flores Silva's capture removes another senior command layer. The arrest is directly relevant to the Sinaloa civil war: CJNG had deployed hundreds of fighters to support Los Chapitos against La Mayiza since late 2024, particularly along the Sinaloa-Durango border and in contested Culiacán neighborhoods. Flores Silva founded Grupo Delta and Grupo Élite — CJNG's specialized shock forces that were the primary external military support for the Chapitos in Sinaloa. With El Mencho dead, Valencia González consolidating internally, and Flores Silva now in custody, the CJNG-Chapitos tactical alliance faces its deepest structural crisis, further deteriorating the Chapitos' external military support. El Jardinero faces extradition to the United States.

media.caption: CJNG's El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) captured in Nayarit on April 29, 2026 — El Mencho's former security chief and potential successor, faces US extradition
